data.title: Microsoft likely to avoid large antitrust fine from EU after Office-Teams offer: report
data.description:
• Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) is likely to avoid being penalized a hefty amount from the European Union after its latest offer on its Office and Teams products, Reuters reported.
  • Some proposals from the Windows maker include better interoperability terms and make it easier for the competition, the news outlet added, citing three people familiar with the matter.
  • The European Commission is still likely to talk to rivals and customers over the next few months before a final decision is made, the people added.
  • Microsoft came under fire when Slack, owned by Salesforce (CRM), complained in 2020 to the E.C. that Microsoft received an unfair advantage by bundling Office and Teams together.
  • Microsoft and the European Commission did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: AbbVie wins approval of Emrelis for non-small cell lung cancer
data.description:
• The U.S. FDA on Wednesday granted accelerated approval to AbbVie's (NYSE:ABBV) Emrelis (telisotuzumab vedotin), an antibody-drug conjugate that is a second-line therapy for non-squamous non-small cell lung cancer with high c-Met protein overexpression.
  • Approval was based on results from a phase 2 trial that showed patients treated with Emrelis showed improvements in overall response rate and duration of response.
  • AbbVie said that Emrelis is the first treatment approved for previously treated advanced NSCLC patients with high c-Met protein overexpression.
  • The FDA also approved Roche's (OTCQX:RHHBY) VENTANA MET (SP44) RxDx Assay, a companion diagnostic that identifies patients suited for treatment with Emrelis.

data.title: Nyxoah outlines U.S. Genio launch plan with 50-member team as FDA approval expected Q2 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Nyxoah (NYXH) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Olivier Taelman emphasized the pivotal progress toward full PMA approval in the United States, confirming, "the FDA issued an approvable letter for our Genio system which confirms that our application substantially meets the requirements for marketing in the U.S." He reported the successful completion and FDA acceptance of required manufacturing validation, stating, "FDA reviewed this validation already and has confirmed they have no further questions regarding it." The final step is an on-site inspection at the U.S. contract manufacturing facility, which is ongoing and previously passed without deficiencies. Taelman expressed confidence in receiving PMA approval in Q2 2025 and outlined the U.S. launch strategy: "We are ready to launch in the U.S. with 50 commercial team members who are all hired and trained." The strategy focuses on targeting high-volume hypoglossal nerve stimulation (HGNS) centers and developing networks with sleep physicians managing moderate to severe obstructive sleep apnea (OSA) populations.

  • Taelman described the approach as a "two-pronged" strategy with a "smart follower" model, leveraging demand for alternatives to pacemaker-based technologies and differentiating on Genio’s unique features, including bilateral stimulation and a battery-free device. The company highlighted the readiness of U.S. teams in sales, marketing, reimbursement, and medical affairs, along with ongoing efforts for payer education and the use of CPT code 64568 for reimbursement.

  • CFO John Landry stated, "We recorded revenue of €1.1 million in the first quarter of 2025 compared to €1.2 million in the first quarter of 2024... The slight year-over-year revenue decrease was primarily due to temporary softness in the international HGNS market." He noted a total operating loss of €20.6 million, attributed to U.S. commercial investments, and reported a cash position of €63 million at March 31, 2025.

Outlook

  • Management reiterated confidence in receiving FDA PMA approval in the second quarter of 2025. Taelman stated, "we continue to expect to receive PMA approval in the second quarter of 2025."

  • The company plans to scale the U.S. commercial team each quarter post-approval and expects pricing to be aligned with competitors, supported by Genio’s technology differentiation. Landry indicated that full-year R&D spending is projected to be in line with 2024, while sales and marketing expenses are expected to be "a little bit more than double what we had incurred in 2024."

Financial Results

  • Landry reported revenue of €1.1 million for Q1 2025 and an operating loss of €20.6 million, with a cash position of €63 million at quarter-end. The revenue dip was linked to temporary international market softness and a phased rollout of the Genio 2.1 software upgrade, which led some sites to defer purchases until its availability. Landry explained, "We expect these factors will normalise in the upcoming quarters."

Q&A

  • Jon Block, Stifel, asked about the ongoing FDA inspection. Taelman explained, "the site inspection is actively taking place as we speak... After this, he will finish his report... And that's also why we stay confident that we will obtain the approval by the end of the quarter."

  • Adam Maeder, Piper Sandler, inquired about statutory response times for FDA approval and the DREAM Journal publication. Taelman clarified, "there is currently an inspection that is taking place... We are feeling confident that we will obtain the FDA approval." He also indicated that DREAM publication is in review and expected before summer.

  • Maeder asked about OpEx pace. Landry responded, "we've taken steps to defer costs and the investments until our FDA approval is achieved... extend our cash runway as well into the second quarter of 2026."

  • Ross Osborn, Cantor Fitzgerald, queried about Genio’s Dubai launch and ACCCESS study. Taelman described milestone implants in Dubai and ongoing expansion in the Middle East; for ACCCESS, "we keep making great progress in enrolling patients... stopping the enrollment by summer."

  • Suraj Kalia, Oppenheimer, asked about inventory management and DTC approach. Taelman stated inventory from competitors would not hinder Genio’s rollout and outlined a focused, non-mass-market DTC strategy.

  • David Rescott, Baird, asked about Genio 2.1 rollout and reimbursement execution. Taelman and Landry described the patient-centric software and readiness for U.S. launch with the established CPT code.

  • Michael Polark, Wolfe Research, sought clarity on CCC labeling. Taelman clarified that CCC would not be a contraindication, but also not yet on-label.

  • Laura Roba, Degroof Petercam, requested more on cash runway levers and market softness. Landry cited deferred discretionary spending and expects international market softness to be temporary.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were focused on clarity around FDA approval timing, commercial strategy, cash runway, and product differentiation, with a tone that was neutral to slightly positive, seeking reassurance on regulatory and operational execution.

  • Management maintained a consistently confident and measured tone, particularly in responses about FDA interactions and U.S. launch readiness, often reiterating timelines and expressing certainty: "we are feeling confident that we will obtain the FDA approval."

  • Compared to the previous quarter, sentiment remained steady with management sustaining high confidence, and analysts maintaining a wait-and-see approach, particularly on regulatory timing and commercial execution.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • FDA approval timing shifted from an expectation by end of March 2025 in Q4 to Q2 2025 in Q1, reflecting the ongoing site inspection. The commercial launch plan remains consistent, with the U.S. team fully prepared and 50 commercial hires in place both quarters. The company continued to highlight the same reimbursement code and two-pronged market entry strategy. Financially, Q1 revenue saw a slight decline from Q1 2024, attributed to temporary international market softness and delayed purchases pending the Genio 2.1 software upgrade. Operating loss increased quarter-over-quarter, driven by U.S. launch investments. Analyst focus remained on regulatory progress, cash runway, and commercial preparedness, while management confidence in the U.S. opportunity and regulatory outcome persisted.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged temporary softness in the international HGNS market and attributed the Q1 revenue dip to this and the Genio 2.1 phased rollout. They expect normalization in coming quarters. Landry stated, "We also saw our competitor in the first quarter also posted a mid-single-digit decline in the international market. We saw some softness there, seasonality. We expect it to be temporary." Discretionary spending has been deferred to extend the cash runway. The remaining risk centers on the pending FDA on-site inspection and subsequent approval timeline.

Final Takeaway

Nyxoah's management reiterated strong confidence in securing FDA approval for Genio in Q2 2025, with all U.S. commercial infrastructure in place for an immediate launch. Temporary revenue softness in international markets is expected to recover, and significant resources have been allocated to ensure a successful U.S. entry. The company is positioning Genio as a differentiated, patient-centric solution with robust clinical data, supported by established reimbursement pathways and a highly trained team, aiming to drive substantial growth after regulatory clearance.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: SA Asks: Can UnitedHealth turn it around?
data.description:
UnitedHealth Group's (NYSE:UNH) stock has plunged nearly 40% year-to-date as the healthcare company has grappled with crisis after crisis, including the aftermath of a massive cyberattack, the murder of a top executive, unexpectedly higher costs, and federal investigations into its business practices.

Shares of the managed care giant were battered yet again on Tuesday after the company withdrew its 2025 financial guidance and announced that CEO Andrew Witty had resigned. Witty will be succeeded by UnitedHealth Chairman Stephen Hemsley, who served as also CEO of the company from 2006 to 2017.

So, what's next for UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH)? Can the healthcare juggernaut turn it around?

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts JR Research, Investing Group Leader for Ultimate Growth Investing, and Dr. Christopher Davis of Quad 7 Capital for their thoughts on what lies ahead.

JR Research: UnitedHealth (UNH) stunned investors by changing its CEO abruptly, although the transition is assessed to be "less disruptive" as new CEO Stephen Hemsley has proved himself as he previously led UNH between 2006 and 2017 and is also its current chair. During his tenure as CEO, Hemsley also helped shape UnitedHealth (UNH) into the monolith that we know today. 

It remains to be seen, however, whether Hemsley's return can reinvigorate a remarkable turnaround amidst the current regulatory headwinds, pricing and utilization challenges emanating from its aggressive enrollment growth. With management maintaining its confidence of returning UNH to its long-term growth trajectory of between 13% to 16%, I believe the UNH story isn't dead yet, suggesting investors must focus on execution over the next two to three quarters to assess the plausible turnaround opportunity further. 

Quad 7 Capital: After this last kitchen sink flush, we highlighted that UNH was in chaos, but presented a potential generational entry opportunity into a world class, blue chip stock. 

Look, a surprise overnight CEO exit and uncertainty around WHY the company is experiencing high costs, on top of its Q1 report and guidance cut, deserved a selloff. But the stock is incredibly cheap historically. Bringing back a tremendous leader in Stephen Hemsley is bullish. He will right the ship. 

We are not saying this stock returns to $500-$600 in a few weeks. But we do think the stock finds a base at around $300. We suggested readers consider buying in $15 drops on the day of the flush. We think that while catching the bottom is improbable, it is possible; this is the type of selloff you have to take advantage of. 

We expect as early as the Q2 earnings we will get some sort of guidance. If not, then in Q3. We will get more clarity on the costs and the plan to address it. For traders with a medium-term horizon, look for a mean reversion trade back over $350-$360 later this year, provided the outlook does not change to project no growth in 2026.

data.title: Wex, Adobe, Sea pitched as long ideas at Sohn Conference
data.description:
Lauren Taylor Wolfe, co-founder and managing partner at Impactive Capital, said payments company Wex (NYSE:WEX) can boost its value with better management.

She presented her idea on Wednesday at the Sohn Investment Conference in New York for charity. Impactive earlier this month announced its intention to vote again three directors at the annual meeting. 

William Heard, chief executive and chief investment officer at Heard Capital, recommends buying shares of Adobe (NASDAQ:ADBE). The software company is poised to win as content creators adopt artificial intelligence, he said.

John Yetimoglu, chief investment officer at Infinitum Asset Management, recommends shares of Southeast Asian tech company Sea Limited (NYSE:SE). The company owns Shopee, which has 55% market share in the region's e-commerce market. Sea shares have 30% upside this year, he added. Sea shares rose 5% on Wednesday.

David Rosen, founder of Rubric Capital, recommends buying shares of media company Canal+.

 
--reported by Rob Williams in New York.

data.title: Creative Realities targets 15% adjusted EBITDA margin by year-end 2025 as national QSR rollout and new platform gains momentum
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Creative Realities, Inc. (CREX) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Rick Mills, CEO, detailed that "we posted revenue of $9.7 million this quarter versus $12.3 million in Q1 of 2024. As I previously discussed, this revenue decrease is a direct result of installation timing, on several large projects. We expect increased revenue as the year progresses."
  • Mills emphasized the importance of a newly secured partnership: "CRI was selected by a well-known upscale quick service restaurant chain with over 1,000 locations... After a successful pilot, which will begin in select locations during the third quarter of 2025, national rollout is expected to proceed." He noted 600 locations have already indicated interest or signed up to convert to digital.
  • The CEO outlined expectations for the AdLogic CPM platform, stating, "this innovative solution provides customers with the tool to deliver targeted high-performance campaigns at significantly reduced cost... It positions CRI's unique one-stop shop for hardware deployments and array of day 2 services and the required ad tech solutions with new monetization models for the company and the customer."
  • Mills added, "we remain on track for another year of record performance. We continue to expect revenue to accelerate beginning in Q2 and particularly in the second half of the year. We also expect adjusted EBITDA as a percentage of revenue to rise to 15% by year-end."
  • Interim CFO Ryan Mudd reported, "as of March 31, 2025, the company had cash on hand of approximately $1.1 million versus $1 million at the end of 2024... Our gross and net debt stood at approximately $23.2 million and $22.1 million, respectively, at the end of the first quarter as compared to $13 million and $12 million, respectively at the start of 2025."
  • Mudd explained the debt rise was tied to the settlement of a contingent liability from the 2022 Reflex Systems merger, resolved for $3 million in cash, a $4 million promissory note, and warrants.
  • Mills also highlighted expansion in sports and entertainment, noting three MLB projects awarded and seven POCs at other venues, as well as ongoing BCTV and Digi Point Media Network deployments.

Outlook

  • Management reiterated, "we remain on track for another year of record performance" and projected that "revenue will accelerate beginning in Q2 and particularly in the second half of the year."
  • The company expects "adjusted EBITDA as a percentage of revenue to rise to 15% by year-end."
  • Mills stated, "we are engaged in numerous opportunities that will lead to backlog growth, revenue predictability and improved margins, even as we look to make headway strengthening our balance sheet through debt reduction whenever possible."
  • The AdLogic CPM platform is expected to drive new monetization models, with updates on customer adoption anticipated in coming months.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q1 2025 was $9.7 million, with gross profit of $4.5 million and gross margin of 46%.
  • Annual recurring revenue (ARR) was at a run rate of $17.3 million at the end of the quarter.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $0.5 million, described as "nominally changed versus last year and the previous quarter due to our active management of underlying overhead costs," with SG&A expenses down 11% year-over-year to $5.2 million.
  • Cash on hand at quarter end was $1.1 million. Gross and net debt increased to $23.2 million and $22.1 million, respectively, mainly due to the contingent liability settlement and working capital needs.

Q&A

  • Brian Kinstlinger, Alliance Global Partners, asked about screen installs for the QSR win and franchisee interest. Mills responded that POCs are underway, with 20+ locations per month expected to start at the end of Q3, and about 600 locations having indicated interest; "Overall, we look at it as -- is it a 2-year project? Probably not probably 3 years. So probably about 300 sites a year, ballpark-ish."
  • Kinstlinger inquired about delays in Q1. Mills explained there were three separate projects causing delays but stated, "We are through that period. We're on track and feel very comfortable on a go-forward basis."
  • Kinstlinger asked about pipeline momentum amid global trade uncertainty. Mills said, "our industry...has not yet been terribly affected by tariffs. All the screen manufacturers' product comes out of Mexico...As of now, we don't have any customer that says, 'Hey, I'm putting this project on hold because of tariffs.'"
  • On ad tech progress, Mills noted, "retail media networks is very much in the early game...We see our ad tech having significant impact – significant potential impact from a revenue perspective in 2026 and 2027."
  • Cal Bartyzal, Craig-Hallum, questioned investments for second half momentum. Mills described facility upgrades and increased warehouse capacity, stating, "we believe we need that for the second half of this year." Technology investments continue but no significant CapEx is anticipated.
  • On sports and entertainment, Mills emphasized high spending appetite and potential for predictable revenue. He also highlighted the Digi Point Media Network as adopting CRI's full tech stack, expecting incremental revenue and SaaS growth.
  • Howard Halpern, Taglich Brothers, asked about opportunities in Mexico. Mills revealed a POC with a top three C-store chain and discussions with major retailers and stadiums, seeing revenue potential in 2026.
  • Mills addressed investor questions about the sales pipeline, indicating 30-40 RFPs per year with a 70% win rate among those that proceed, though deployment can be delayed by customers' capital cycles.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was neutral to slightly positive, focusing on project timelines, customer demand, and strategic clarity. Questions highlighted interest in pipeline conversion, QSR rollout execution, and the impact of tariffs.
  • Management's tone was confident in both prepared remarks and Q&A, with Mills stating, "we are through that period. We're on track and feel very comfortable on a go-forward basis." Slight caution was noted regarding macro uncertainty, but overall, management consistently projected optimism about growth and operational execution.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts in both calls remained primarily inquisitive; management's tone shifted from cautious optimism to more assertive confidence about pipeline momentum and operational improvements.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from general optimism for 2025 to more specific expectations of revenue acceleration beginning in Q2 and an explicit 15% adjusted EBITDA margin target by year-end.
  • Strategic focus sharpened with the announcement of a major QSR win and broader adoption of the AdLogic CPM platform.
  • Key financial metrics in Q1 2025 showed a revenue and gross profit decline from Q4 2024, but adjusted EBITDA and SG&A cost management stabilized profitability.
  • Management's confidence was higher in the current quarter, emphasizing cleared project delays and expanded opportunity pipeline.
  • Analysts continued to focus on project execution and pipeline conversion; their tone remained consistent with prior quarters.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged installation delays in three separate projects but stated the issues have been resolved.
  • Tariff-related uncertainty was discussed, with macro risks to hardware supply chains and steel pricing, though no material customer impacts have been observed to date.
  • The company cited rising debt levels due to contingent liability settlement and increased working capital but emphasized ongoing efforts to optimize the capital structure and prioritize debt reduction.
  • Possible project timing risk remains, particularly in large-scale deployments and retail media network adoption cycles.

Final Takeaway

Creative Realities' management reported cleared project delays, a strengthened sales pipeline, and a major new QSR partnership that is expected to drive meaningful growth beginning in the second half of 2025. With a focus on operational execution, cost control, and new monetization avenues through its AdLogic CPM platform, the company targets a 15% adjusted EBITDA margin by year-end and anticipates expanded revenue predictability and margin improvement as the year progresses.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Trump DEA Administrator nominee sidesteps marijuana rescheduling questions
data.description:
Terrance Cole, President Trump's nominee for DEA Administrator, largely sidestepped questions from two Democratic senators regarding marijuana reforms.

Sens. Cory Booker (N.J.) and Alex Padilla (Calif.) asked Cole his position on whether he agrees with a proposal to move cannabis from Schedule I to Schedule III under the Controlled Substances Act (CSA). In a written response, Cole wrote that he would "give the matter careful consideration after consulting with appropriate personnel within the Drug Enforcement Administration, familiarizing myself with the current status of the regulatory process, and reviewing all relevant information."

He was also asked whether he felt the DEA is required to follow the scheduling process as stated in the CSA. "As in all matters, if confirmed, I would look at the individualized facts and circumstances and follow the law and any policies of the Department,” Cole wrote, Marijuana Moment reported.

In a confirmation hearing before the Senate Judiciary Committee in April, Cole noted that he would prioritize an ongoing review at the agency to reclassify marijuana as a less dangerous drug.

Multi-state operators: Ascend Wellness (OTCQX:AAWH), Curaleaf Holdings (OTCPK:CURLF), Cresco Labs (OTCQX:CRLBF), Green Thumb Industries (OTCQX:GTBIF), Trulieve Cannabis (OTCQX:TCNNF), Ayr Wellness (OTCQX:AYRWF), Verano Holdings (OTCQX:VRNOF), and Jushi Holdings (OTCQX:JUSHF).

Cannabis ETFs: AdvisorShares Pure Cannabis ETF (NYSEARCA:YOLO), Amplify Alternative Harvest ETF (NYSEARCA:MJ), and Amplify Seymour Cannabis ETF (NYSEARCA:CNBS).

data.title: PENN Entertainment faces more criticism from activist investor HG Vora over its strategies
data.description:
Activist investor HG Vora Capital Management issued a letter to shareholders of PENN Entertainment (NASDAQ:PENN) that was sharply critical of the company's leadership and strategy.

As one of Penn’s largest shareholders, HG Vora argued that PEN's (NASDAQ:PENN) stock has significantly underperformed gaming peers over the past decade, attributing the underperformance to failed strategic shifts, poor capital allocation, and mismanagement. 

The letter accuses the current PENN (PENN) board and management, particularly CEO Jay Snowden and Board Chair David Handler, of eroding shareholder value through costly and unsuccessful acquisitions such as Score Media, Barstool Sports, and the ESPN Bet partnership. Despite the investments, PENN's (PENN) online sports betting platform was noted to only hold a 2% market share, while executive compensation has risen dramatically/

HG Vora also alleged that the board has taken steps to entrench itself and avoid accountability, including reducing the number of board seats up for election after HG Vora nominated three independent directors. The letter claims the move is both legally questionable and a breach of fiduciary duty, prompting HG Vora to initiate litigation to restore shareholders' rights.

The activist investor urges shareholders to vote for all three of its independent nominees, arguing that only a reconstituted board can restore accountability and refocus PENN (PENN) on its core strengths in regional casinos and omnichannel gaming.

HG Vora holds a 4.8% stake in PENN.

If HG Vora's campaign succeeds, PENN (PENN) could see a shift in board dynamics, renewed scrutiny of capital allocation and strategy, and potentially a reversal of its digital expansion in favor of its traditional casino operations. The date for the 2025 annual meeting is June 17. BlackRock Advisors is the top shareholder, holding approximately 11.1% of the company's shares. Vanguard Group Inc. follows closely with about 10.7%.

data.title: Evolution Petroleum outlines $850 BOE/day boost from new wells and Tex-Mex acquisition while delaying Chaveroo Block 3 amid volatile oil prices
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Evolution Petroleum Corporation (EPM) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Kelly Loyd, President & CEO, stated that "Our fiscal third quarter results demonstrated Evolution's commitment to disciplined capital allocation and strategic execution. We stayed grounded in our core strengths, allocating capital prudently to high-quality, low-decline assets, maintaining our long-standing dividend and generating positive cash flow." Loyd highlighted the closure of the Tex-Mex acquisition and the addition of four new wells in the second Chaveroo block, noting, "Together, these additions are currently contributing more than 850 net barrels of oil equivalent per day and are expected to meaningfully benefit our fiscal fourth quarter production and cash flow, especially when coupled with the recent strength in natural gas pricing." He emphasized the Tex-Mex transaction's $9 million valuation at "approximately 3.4 times forward adjusted EBITDA" and described the acquisition as "highly accretive... even amid recent oil price volatility."
  • Loyd explained the decision to delay the third Chaveroo development block, saying, "In light of the recent market volatility, we... have made the decision to delay the start of our third development block to later into our fiscal year 2026." He added, "We believe it's prudent to now focus our development activities toward gas-weighted opportunities particularly in the SCOOP/STACK."
  • On dividends, Loyd stated, "On May 12, our Board declared a cash dividend of $0.12 per share of common stock, marking our 47th consecutive quarter of issuing a dividend and our 12th consecutive quarter at $0.12 per share."
  • Mark Bunch, COO, reported, "At SCOOP/STACK, 13 gross wells have come online fiscal year-to-date. We have another five gross wells in progress." He also noted that the four new Chaveroo wells were "drilled and completed on schedule and under budget."
  • Ryan Stash, CFO, said, "In fiscal Q3, we had total revenues of $22.6 million, down 2% year-over-year. The decline was driven primarily by an 8% decrease in production volumes, partially offset by a 7% increase in average realized commodity prices, driven by stronger natural gas and NGL prices." Stash highlighted, "Net loss for the third quarter was $2.2 million or $0.07 per share compared to net income of $0.3 million or $0.01 per share in the year ago period."

Outlook

  • Management indicated that "the fruits of our disciplined acquisition and development strategy during fiscal Q3 will be made obvious in our fiscal fourth quarter when we will see the effects of our Tex-Mex acquisition and our four new Chaveroo wells begin to contribute to our quarterly results."
  • Loyd said the company is "actively pursuing opportunities in what we view as a highly attractive market to acquire oil-weighted load decline producing assets or natural gas properties with favorable hedging potential."
  • The decision to delay Chaveroo Block 3 into fiscal 2026 was described as a response to oil market volatility, with a stated focus on gas-weighted opportunities in the near term.

Financial Results

  • Stash reported, "Adjusted EBITDA was $7.4 million compared to $8.5 million in the year ago period. The decrease was primarily due to lower revenue as a result of lower production volumes and higher total operating costs due to CO2 purchases at Delhi."
  • Cash and cash equivalents totaled $5.6 million and borrowings outstanding under the revolving credit facility were $35.5 million. Total liquidity was $20.1 million at quarter end.
  • The company paid $4.1 million in common stock dividends, made $4 million in repayments on its senior secured credit facility, and incurred $4.4 million in capital expenditures during the quarter.
  • Stash announced, "We have received approval from our lender MidFirst Bank to extend the maturity of our facility to April 2028 and increased their total commitments from $50 million to $55 million. Also, we expect to receive $10 million in additional commitments from a new lender, Prism Bank, which will bring our total commitments to $65 million."

Q&A

  • Jeff Grampp, Northland Capital Markets: Questioned M&A market dynamics and trends separating oil- and gas-weighted acquisitions. Loyd responded that current market conditions favor acquiring low-decline assets, especially with expectations of oil price recovery: "...if oil is going to follow the strip...domestic crude oil production is going to decline...the only way to do that is be the price mechanism, so prices will go higher."
  • Grampp asked about Chaveroo well performance. Bunch replied, "We're about 7 miles away from the 500 wells...these wells have come on really, really good. They're probably about 50% high right now to what we expected them to be producing on average."
  • Chris Degner, Water Tower Research: Asked about the shift to waterflood at Delhi. Loyd answered, "It's on the order of $400,000, $500,000 per month of cost savings to us -- and we don't expect much if any difference in performance."
  • Poe Fratt, Alliance Global Partners: Sought clarification on net production increases and CapEx expectations. Stash indicated it was "a little early for us to put out anything next fiscal year" but reaffirmed the full-year CapEx budget remains valid.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was neutral to slightly positive, focusing on M&A dynamics, well performance, and cost savings, with no aggressive challenges to management.
  • Management expressed cautious optimism, frequently referencing discipline and flexibility. Loyd stated, "We remain well hedged on oil...providing a strong safety net that supports both our CapEx program and dividend," and also acknowledged volatility: "This volatile market environment underscores the value and resiliency of our diversified portfolio."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts maintained a constructive tone, while management's remarks shifted from enthusiasm for organic growth to a more defensive, disciplined posture amid price volatility and operational delays.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter saw the closure of the Tex-Mex acquisition and the online status of four Chaveroo wells, with immediate impacts flagged for Q4.
  • Guidance shifted with the decision to delay Chaveroo Block 3, representing a pivot in development priorities toward gas-weighted assets.
  • Dividend stability continued, but net loss replaced net income compared to the prior year period.
  • Management’s tone was more cautious, referencing market volatility and the need for disciplined capital allocation, while analysts remained focused on growth and cost management.

Risks and Concerns

  • Oil price volatility and its impact on development timing were highlighted, with management delaying oil-weighted projects to preserve cash flow.
  • Operational risks included weather-related and maintenance downtime, particularly at Barnett and Delhi.
  • Management emphasized diversification and hedging as mitigation strategies, stating a focus on "measured development approach in a low price environment" and cost-control initiatives like the Delhi waterflood transition.

Final Takeaway

Evolution Petroleum’s fiscal third quarter was marked by strategic acquisition and operational execution, positioning the company to benefit from new production in the upcoming quarter. Management’s decision to delay oil-weighted development and focus on gas opportunities reflects a disciplined response to commodity price volatility, while stable dividends and increased liquidity signal a commitment to returning value to shareholders and maintaining financial flexibility in an uncertain market environment.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Micron expects demand to remain in-line with earlier expectations despite tariffs: Wells Fargo
data.description:
Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) continues to maintain its previously revealed demand expectations despite tariff concerns and rising competition from China, according to Wells Fargo.

"While MU did not update its F3Q25 guide (we think there has been some expectation of a positive update), we note that MU's overall demand comments were positive, in particular as it relates to Data Center demand trends; industry supply-demand and pricing dynamics," said Wells Fargo analysts, led by Aaron Rakers, in an investor note. "In terms of tariff-driven pull-ins, MU only noted that there could be some, but also pointed to the overall demand expectations previously laid out as in line w/ expectations."

Micron presented at J.P. Morgan's Global Technology, Media and Communications Conference on Wednesday morning. The company produces memory and storage solutions for a wide range of products and data centers. Approximately 55% of Micron's revenue during the first half of fiscal year 2025 stemmed from data centers. 

"In terms of China-domiciled competition, which today are focused on legacy DDR4 solutions, Micron noted that it has seen China competitors in the market—this expectation is factored into the company's long-term supply-demand planning," Rakers added. "Micron pointed out that DDR5 solutions are more complex; company focused on the highest value-added/most complex solutions in the market."

Micron is currently working to start up three fabs in the U.S. The locations include Idaho, New York and Virginia. Its fiscal year capital expenditures are expected to total about $14B.

"Micron's Singapore packaging facility broke ground in January and is expected to meaningfully increase advanced packaging capacity in 2027," Rakers noted. "The company has previously noted >50% of y/y capex increase will be driven by construction capex for Idaho and New York fabs, which would not contribute to meaningful bit supply in F2025 and F2026."

Wells Fargo maintains its Overweight rating and $130 price target on Micron.
